steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes Inez Milholland's early advocacy work at Vassar in 1905, then jumps to her national prominence in 1913. The blank must connect an early start to a later outcome — a forward-in-time, culminating relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'As such,': signals an immediate logical consequence, not a long-term culmination. It suggests the prominence followed directly and automatically, which misses the passage's timeline. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Ultimately,': signals that something happened in the end, after a progression. It correctly captures that her years of advocacy work eventually led to national prominence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'For example,': signals that what follows is an illustration of what came before. Leading the 1913 march is not presented as an example of her causes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Similarly,': signals a parallel or comparison. There is no second situation being compared here. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
